title: Visual automation and Cursor approval boundaries (14 DRP aligned)
principle: No automation shall simulate human input or programmatically bypass Cursor/VS Code approval surfaces (Allowlist, Run, Approve terminal command). Operator consent and platform security outrank convenience.
approved_patterns: Hub dashboard HTML (e.g. port 5502) for supervision and links — human-initiated
Z-Bridge / guardian / operator digest JSON — append-only observability
AAFRTC and Z: tasks — predefined npm pipelines from hub root only
Cursor slash commands and hooks for logging / ritual — not approval forgery
rejected_patterns: RPA or OS-level synthetic mouse/keyboard targeting Cursor UI
Hidden auto-accept of terminal or file-access prompts
Undisclosed remote control of the operator desktop
deeper_sections_in_doc: Legitimate levers (tasks, slash commands, dashboard, hooks logging)
review_cadence: Quarterly or after major Cursor upgrade; re-check data/z_visual_automation_boundary.json against new platform features
